title: A New Pill Offers Hope for Pancreatic Cancer Patients
subtitle: Clinical trial results show a new drug can double survival time for a disease once thought untreatable.

summary.en: A new daily pill called daraxonrasib has shown promising results in treating pancreatic cancer. Clinical trials found that the drug doubles survival time compared to traditional chemotherapy. Experts are calling this discovery a major breakthrough for a disease that has historically been very difficult to treat. The drug works by targeting a specific protein that helps cancer cells grow and spread. While the drug is not yet fully approved, access has been expanded for patients who need it.
summary.zh: 一種名為 daraxonrasib 的新型每日口服藥物在治療胰臟癌方面展現了令人期待的成果。臨床試驗發現，與傳統化學治療相比，該藥物能將存活時間延長一倍。專家稱此發現為該疾病的一大突破，因為胰臟癌在歷史上一直極難治療。該藥物的作用機制是鎖定一種有助於癌細胞生長與擴散的特定蛋白質。雖然該藥物尚未完全獲得核准，但已擴大提供給有需要的患者使用。

paragraphs.en: Pancreatic cancer has long been one of the most difficult diseases for doctors to treat. For many years, patients have faced very low survival rates, and doctors have had few effective options to offer. However, a new clinical trial has provided a reason for hope. According to The Guardian Health, researchers have discovered a daily pill called daraxonrasib that can double the survival time for patients with this deadly disease.
paragraphs.zh: 胰臟癌長期以來一直是醫師最難治療的疾病之一。多年來，患者面臨極低的存活率，醫師也幾乎沒有有效的治療選項。然而，一項新的臨床試驗帶來了希望。根據《衛報健康》（The Guardian Health）報導，研究人員發現了一種名為 daraxonrasib 的每日口服藥物，能讓這種致命疾病患者的存活時間延長一倍。

paragraphs.en: The findings were recently presented at the annual meeting of the American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) in Chicago. In a study involving 500 patients, those who took the new drug lived for an average of 13.2 months. In comparison, patients who received standard chemotherapy lived for only about 6.6 to 6.7 months. Dr. Julie Gralow, the chief medical officer at ASCO, described the results as a “grand slam” for cancer research, noting that it is one of the most significant developments in decades.
paragraphs.zh: 這項研究結果近期在芝加哥舉行的美國臨床腫瘤學會（ASCO）年會上發表。在一項涵蓋五百名患者的研究中，服用新藥的患者平均存活時間為十三點二個月；相較之下，接受標準化學治療的患者僅能存活約六點六至六點七個月。美國臨床腫瘤學會的首席醫療官茱莉·格拉洛（Julie Gralow）博士形容此結果為癌症研究的「全壘打」，並指出這是數十年來最重要的發展之一。

paragraphs.en: To understand why this drug is special, it helps to look at how pancreatic cancer grows. Most cases of this cancer involve a mutation in a gene called KRAS. This gene creates a protein that sends signals to cancer cells, telling them to grow and divide even when they should not. According to MedPage Today, daraxonrasib is a new type of medicine that acts as a "Ras inhibitor." This means it attaches to the KRAS protein and turns it off, effectively stopping the cancer from receiving the signals it needs to spread.
paragraphs.zh: 要了解為何這種藥物如此特別，必須先了解胰臟癌的生長機制。此類癌症多數涉及一種稱為 KRAS 的基因突變。該基因會產生一種蛋白質，向癌細胞發送訊號，促使癌細胞在不該生長時仍持續生長與分裂。根據《今日醫學頁》（MedPage Today）報導，daraxonrasib 是一種新型藥物，屬於「Ras 抑制劑」。這意味著它能附著在 KRAS 蛋白質上並將其關閉，有效阻斷癌細胞接收擴散所需的訊號。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Brian Wolpin, the lead investigator from the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, explained that this drug targets the basic molecular cause of the disease. By stopping the signals that drive tumor growth, the drug allows patients to live significantly longer. Dr. Rachna Shroff, an expert from the University of Arizona Cancer Center, noted that the study proves it is possible to successfully target the KRAS pathway, which has been a major goal for scientists for a long time.
paragraphs.zh: 來自丹娜法伯癌症研究所（Dana-Farber Cancer Institute）的首席研究員布萊恩·沃爾平（Brian Wolpin）博士解釋，這種藥物針對的是疾病最根本的分子成因。透過阻斷驅動腫瘤生長的訊號，該藥物能讓患者存活時間顯著延長。亞利桑那大學癌症中心（University of Arizona Cancer Center）的專家拉赫娜·史洛夫（Rachna Shroff）博士指出，這項研究證明成功鎖定 KRAS 路徑是可行的，而這長期以來一直是科學家追求的主要目標。

paragraphs.en: One of the most important benefits of daraxonrasib is that it is easier for patients to handle than traditional treatments. Chemotherapy is often very harsh, causing side effects that make it difficult for patients to continue their treatment. NPR reports that while the new pill can cause a mild rash, it does not have the severe, exhausting side effects associated with chemotherapy. This is important because it not only helps patients live longer, but it also improves the quality of their daily lives.
paragraphs.zh: daraxonrasib 最重要的優點之一，是患者比傳統治療更容易適應。化學治療通常非常劇烈，會產生讓患者難以持續治療的副作用。據美國公共廣播電台（NPR）報導，雖然這種新藥可能會引起輕微皮疹，但不會出現化學治療常見的那種嚴重且令人精疲力竭的副作用。這一點至關重要，因為它不僅能幫助患者延長壽命，還能改善他們的日常生活品質。

paragraphs.en: Despite these positive results, pancreatic cancer remains a serious challenge. According to NPR, about 70,000 people in the United States are diagnosed with this disease every year. Because the cancer is often found late, after it has already spread to other parts of the body, it has been historically difficult to manage. The new study focused on patients whose cancer had already spread, making the survival improvements even more impressive.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管有這些正面結果，胰臟癌仍是一項嚴峻的挑戰。根據美國公共廣播電台報導，美國每年約有七萬人被診斷出罹患此病。由於胰臟癌通常在發現時已晚，且癌細胞已擴散至身體其他部位，因此在歷史上一直難以控制。這項新研究聚焦於癌症已經擴散的患者，這使得存活率的提升顯得更加令人印象深刻。

paragraphs.en: Currently, the drug is not yet fully approved for general use, but it is becoming more available. MedPage Today reports that the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has allowed the maker of the drug to start an expanded access program. This program allows doctors to provide the medicine to patients who need it before the final approval process is finished. This is a common step for promising new treatments that show clear benefits in early testing.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，該藥物尚未完全獲得全面使用的核准，但取得管道正逐漸增加。《今日醫學頁》報導指出，美國食品藥物管理局（FDA）已允許該藥廠啟動一項擴大使用計畫。此計畫允許醫師在最終核准程序完成前，將藥物提供給有需要的患者。對於在早期測試中展現明確效益的潛力新療法而言，這是一項常見的步驟。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, experts believe this discovery marks the beginning of a new era in cancer care. Dr. Wolpin noted that as researchers learn more about the molecular basis of diseases, they will be able to create more effective, targeted treatments. For patients and their families, this new pill represents a significant step forward in the fight against a disease that was once considered almost impossible to treat.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，專家認為這項發現標誌著癌症照護新時代的開端。沃爾平博士指出，隨著研究人員對疾病分子基礎的了解加深，他們將能開發出更有效、更精準的治療方法。對於患者及其家屬而言，這種新藥代表在對抗一種曾被認為幾乎無法治療的疾病時，邁出了重要的一步。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A major success or a significant achievement.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 巨大的成功、重大的成就。
keyTerms.noteZh: 原指體育賽事中的大滿貫，這裡比喻極具突破性的研究成果。文中：Dr. Julie Gralow, the chief medical officer at ASCO, described the results as a “grand slam” for cancer research, noting that it is one of the most significant developments in decades.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To cope with or endure something difficult.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（把手）或動詞（處理），這裡指病人對治療副作用的耐受程度。文中：One of the most important benefits of daraxonrasib is that it is easier for patients to handle than traditional treatments.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To cause or force something to happen or develop.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（駕駛），這裡指推動腫瘤生長的生理機制。文中：By stopping the signals that drive tumor growth, the drug allows patients to live significantly longer.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Daily pill can double survival time for world’s deadliest cancer, trial shows

sources.name: NPR Health
sources.title: Pancreatic cancer was once considered almost untreatable. A new drug is offering hope

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: 'A Grand Slam' in Pancreatic Cancer: Novel Pill Doubles Survival

quiz.question: According to the study results presented at the ASCO meeting, how does the survival time of patients taking daraxonrasib compare to those undergoing standard chemotherapy?

options.A: It is exactly twice as long as the 6.6 months observed in chemotherapy patients.
options.B: It averages 13.2 months, which is roughly double the duration of standard chemotherapy.
options.C: It is slightly less than double the 6.7 months seen in traditional treatment groups.
options.D: It provides an additional 6.7 months of life compared to the 13.2 months of chemotherapy.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，研究中服用新藥的患者平均存活期為 13.2 個月，而接受化療的患者為 6.6 到 6.7 個月，13.2 確實約為 6.6 到 6.7 的兩倍。選項 A 錯誤，因為 6.6 的兩倍是 13.2，但文中給出的化療範圍是 6.6 到 6.7，不能說「剛好」是 6.6 的兩倍。選項 C 錯誤，因為 13.2 明顯大於 6.7 的兩倍（13.4）。選項 D 則是將數據順序搞混了，化療的時間並非 13.2 個月。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the patients who participated in the daraxonrasib clinical trial?

options.A: They were all in the early stages of pancreatic cancer before the trial began.
options.B: They had previously failed all other available cancer treatments.
options.C: Their cancer had already advanced beyond the pancreas to other parts of the body.
options.D: They were specifically chosen because they had no KRAS gene mutations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第六段，文中提到該研究針對的是癌症已經擴散（spread）的患者，並強調這使得存活率的提升更加令人印象深刻。選項 A 與文意相反，因為癌症已擴散通常代表非早期。選項 B 文中未提及所有患者都嘗試過其他療法。選項 D 錯誤，因為文章第三段說明該藥物是針對 KRAS 基因突變，若患者沒有此突變，藥物可能無效。

quiz.question: In the second paragraph, Dr. Julie Gralow describes the study results as a “grand slam.” What does this expression mean in this context?

options.A: A violent and aggressive attack on cancer cells.
options.B: A highly successful and significant achievement.
options.C: A final attempt to save a patient's life.
options.D: A complicated procedure that requires multiple steps.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。「Grand slam」原意為棒球中的滿貫全壘打，在文中用來比喻這項研究取得了極大的成功與突破。選項 A 將其誤解為攻擊性行為。選項 C 誤以為是「最後一搏」，但文中語氣是正面的突破而非絕望的嘗試。選項 D 則完全偏離了該詞彙在體育與商業比喻中代表「巨大成功」的意涵。

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of this article?

options.A: To explain the biological process of how pancreatic cancer spreads through the body.
options.B: To criticize the harsh side effects caused by traditional chemotherapy treatments.
options.C: To discuss the FDA's new regulations regarding the approval of experimental cancer drugs.
options.D: To report on a promising new drug that offers improved survival and quality of life for pancreatic cancer patients.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。整篇文章的核心在於介紹 daraxonrasib 這款新藥，說明它如何透過抑制 KRAS 蛋白質來延長患者壽命，並改善生活品質。選項 A 只是文章用來解釋藥物機制的背景資訊，而非主旨。選項 B 雖然有提到化療的副作用，但這只是用來襯托新藥優點的次要論點。選項 C 僅是文章結尾提到的輔助資訊，並非全文討論的重點。
